    68→// DeploymentController implements a controller that materializes a Gateway into an in cluster gateway proxy
    69→// to serve requests from. This is implemented with a Deployment and Service today.
    70→// The implementation makes a few non-obvious choices - namely using Server Side Apply from go templates
    71→// and not using controller-runtime.
    72→//
    73→// controller-runtime has a number of constraints that make it inappropriate for usage here, despite this
    74→// seeming to be the bread and butter of the library:
    75→// * It is not readily possible to bring existing Informers, which would require extra watches (#1668)
    76→// * Goroutine leaks (#1655)
    77→// * Excessive API-server calls at startup which have no benefit to us (#1603)
    78→// * Hard to use with SSA (#1669)
    79→// While these can be worked around, at some point it isn't worth the effort.
    80→//
    81→// Server Side Apply with go templates is an odd choice (no one likes YAML templating...) but is one of the few
    82→// remaining options after all others are ruled out.
    83→//   - Merge patch/Update cannot be used. If we always enforce that our object is *exactly* the same as
    84→//     the in-cluster object we will get in endless loops due to other controllers that like to add annotations, etc.
    85→//     If we chose to allow any unknown fields, then we would never be able to remove fields we added, as
    86→//     we cannot tell if we created it or someone else did. SSA fixes these issues
    87→//   - SSA using client-go Apply libraries is almost a good choice, but most third-party clients (Istio, MCS, and gateway-api)
    88→//     do not provide these libraries.
    89→//   - SSA using standard API types doesn't work well either: https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/controller-runtime/issues/1669
    90→//   - This leaves YAML templates, converted to unstructured types and Applied with the dynamic client.
    91→type DeploymentController struct {
    92→	client              kube.Client
    93→	clusterID           cluster.ID
    94→	env                 *model.Environment
    95→	queue               controllers.Queue
    96→	patcher             patcher
    97→	gateways            kclient.Client[*gateway.Gateway]
    98→	gatewayClasses      kclient.Client[*gateway.GatewayClass]
    99→	listenerSets        kclient.Client[*gatewayx.XListenerSet]
   100→	listenerSetByParent kclient.Index[types.NamespacedName, *gatewayx.XListenerSet]
   101→
   102→	clients         map[schema.GroupVersionResource]getter
   103→	injectConfig    func() inject.WebhookConfig
   104→	deployments     kclient.Client[*appsv1.Deployment]
   105→	services        kclient.Client[*corev1.Service]
   106→	hpas            kclient.Client[*autoscalingv2.HorizontalPodAutoscaler]
   107→	pdbs            kclient.Client[*policyv1.PodDisruptionBudget]
   108→	configMaps      kclient.Client[*corev1.ConfigMap]
   109→	serviceAccounts kclient.Client[*corev1.ServiceAccount]
   110→	namespaces      kclient.Client[*corev1.Namespace]
   111→	tagWatcher      revisions.TagWatcher
   112→	revision        string
   113→	systemNamespace string
   114→}
   115→
   116→// Patcher is a function that abstracts patching logic. This is largely because client-go fakes do not handle patching
   117→type patcher func(gvr schema.GroupVersionResource, name string, namespace string, data []byte, subresources ...string) error
   118→
   119→// classInfo holds information about a gateway class
   120→type classInfo struct {
   121→	// controller name for this class
   122→	controller string
   123→	// controller label for this class
   124→	controllerLabel string
   125→	// description for this class
   126→	description string
   127→	// The key in the templates to use for this class
   128→	templates string
   129→
   130→	// defaultServiceType sets the default service type if one is not explicit set
   131→	defaultServiceType corev1.ServiceType
   132→
   133→	// disableRouteGeneration, if set, will make it so the controller ignores this class.
   134→	disableRouteGeneration bool
   135→
   136→	// supportsListenerSet declares whether a given class supports ListenerSet
   137→	supportsListenerSet bool
   138→
   139→	// disableNameSuffix, if set, will avoid appending -<class> to names
   140→	disableNameSuffix bool
   141→
   142→	// addressType is the default address type to report
   143→	addressType gateway.AddressType
   144→}

   146→var classInfos = getClassInfos()
   147→
   148→var builtinClasses = getBuiltinClasses()
   149→
   150→func getBuiltinClasses() map[gateway.ObjectName]gateway.GatewayController {
   151→	res := map[gateway.ObjectName]gateway.GatewayController{
   152→		gateway.ObjectName(features.GatewayAPIDefaultGatewayClass): gateway.GatewayController(features.ManagedGatewayController),
   153→	}
   154→
   155→	if features.MultiNetworkGatewayAPI {
   156→		res[constants.RemoteGatewayClassName] = constants.UnmanagedGatewayController
   157→	}
   158→
   159→	if features.EnableAmbientWaypoints {
   160→		res[constants.WaypointGatewayClassName] = constants.ManagedGatewayMeshController
   161→	}
   162→
   163→	// N.B Ambient e/w gateways are just fancy waypoints, but we want a different
   164→	// GatewayClass for better UX
   165→	if features.EnableAmbientMultiNetwork {
   166→		res[constants.EastWestGatewayClassName] = constants.ManagedGatewayEastWestController
   167→	}
   168→	return res
   169→}
   170→
   171→func getClassInfos() map[gateway.GatewayController]classInfo {
   172→	m := map[gateway.GatewayController]classInfo{
   173→		gateway.GatewayController(features.ManagedGatewayController): {
   174→			controller:          features.ManagedGatewayController,
   175→			description:         "The default Istio GatewayClass",
   176→			templates:           "kube-gateway",
   177→			defaultServiceType:  corev1.ServiceTypeLoadBalancer,
   178→			addressType:         gateway.HostnameAddressType,
   179→			controllerLabel:     constants.ManagedGatewayControllerLabel,
   180→			supportsListenerSet: true,
   181→		},
   182→	}
   183→
   184→	if features.MultiNetworkGatewayAPI {
   185→		m[constants.UnmanagedGatewayController] = classInfo{
   186→			// This represents a gateway that our control plane cannot discover directly via the API server.
   187→			// We shouldn't generate Istio resources for it. We aren't programming this gateway.
   188→			controller:             constants.UnmanagedGatewayController,
   189→			description:            "Remote to this cluster. Does not deploy or affect configuration.",
   190→			disableRouteGeneration: true,
   191→			addressType:            gateway.HostnameAddressType,
   192→			supportsListenerSet:    false,
   193→		}
   194→	}
   195→	if features.EnableAmbientWaypoints {
   196→		m[constants.ManagedGatewayMeshController] = classInfo{
   197→			controller:          constants.ManagedGatewayMeshController,
   198→			description:         "The default Istio waypoint GatewayClass",
   199→			templates:           "waypoint",
   200→			disableNameSuffix:   true,
   201→			defaultServiceType:  corev1.ServiceTypeClusterIP,
   202→			supportsListenerSet: false,
   203→			// Report both. Consumers of the gateways can choose which they want.
   204→			// In particular, Istio across different versions consumes different address types, so this retains compat
   205→			addressType:     "",
   206→			controllerLabel: constants.ManagedGatewayMeshControllerLabel,
   207→		}
   208→	}
   209→
   210→	if features.EnableAmbientMultiNetwork {
   211→		m[constants.ManagedGatewayEastWestController] = classInfo{
   212→			controller:         constants.ManagedGatewayEastWestController,
   213→			description:        "The default GatewayClass for Istio East West Gateways",
   214→			templates:          "waypoint",
   215→			disableNameSuffix:  true,
   216→			defaultServiceType: corev1.ServiceTypeLoadBalancer,
   217→			addressType:        "",
   218→			controllerLabel:    constants.ManagedGatewayEastWestControllerLabel,
   219→		}
   220→	}
   221→	return m
   222→}
   223→
   224→// NewDeploymentController constructs a DeploymentController and registers required informers.
   225→// The controller will not start until Run() is called.
   226→func NewDeploymentController(
   227→	client kube.Client,
   228→	clusterID cluster.ID,
   229→	env *model.Environment,
   230→	webhookConfig func() inject.WebhookConfig,
   231→	injectionHandler func(fn func()),
   232→	tw revisions.TagWatcher,
   233→	revision string,
   234→	systemNamespace string,
   235→) *DeploymentController {
   236→	filter := kclient.Filter{ObjectFilter: client.ObjectFilter()}
   237→	gateways := kclient.NewFiltered[*gateway.Gateway](client, filter)
   238→	gatewayClasses := kclient.New[*gateway.GatewayClass](client)
   239→	dc := &DeploymentController{
   240→		client:    client,
   241→		clusterID: clusterID,
   242→		clients:   map[schema.GroupVersionResource]getter{},
   243→		env:       env,
   244→		patcher: func(gvr schema.GroupVersionResource, name string, namespace string, data []byte, subresources ...string) error {
   245→			c := client.Dynamic().Resource(gvr).Namespace(namespace)
   246→			t := true
   247→			_, err := c.Patch(context.Background(), name, types.ApplyPatchType, data, metav1.PatchOptions{
   248→				Force:        &t,
   249→				FieldManager: features.ManagedGatewayController,
   250→			}, subresources...)
   251→			return err
   252→		},
   253→		gateways:        gateways,
   254→		gatewayClasses:  gatewayClasses,
   255→		injectConfig:    webhookConfig,
   256→		tagWatcher:      tw,
   257→		revision:        revision,
   258→		systemNamespace: systemNamespace,
   259→	}
   260→	gatewaysByParamsRef := kclient.CreateIndex(gateways, "parametersRef", func(o *gateway.Gateway) []types.NamespacedName {
   261→		p, err := fetchParameters(o)
   262→		if p == nil || err != nil {
   263→			return nil
   264→		}
   265→		return []types.NamespacedName{*p}
   266→	})
   267→	dc.queue = controllers.NewQueue("gateway deployment",
   268→		controllers.WithReconciler(dc.Reconcile),
   269→		controllers.WithMaxAttempts(5))
   270→
   271→	if features.EnableAlphaGatewayAPI {
   272→		dc.listenerSets = kclient.NewFiltered[*gatewayx.XListenerSet](client, filter)
   273→		dc.listenerSetByParent = kclient.CreateIndex(dc.listenerSets, "parent", func(o *gatewayx.XListenerSet) []types.NamespacedName {
   274→			return []types.NamespacedName{extractListenerSetParent(o)}
   275→		})
   276→		dc.listenerSets.AddEventHandler(controllers.TypedObjectHandler(func(o *gatewayx.XListenerSet) {
   277→			dc.queue.Add(extractListenerSetParent(o))
   278→		}))
   279→	}
   280→
   281→	// Set up a handler that will add the parent Gateway object onto the queue.
   282→	// The queue will only handle Gateway objects; if child resources (Service, etc) are updated we re-add
   283→	// the Gateway to the queue and reconcile the state of the world.
   284→	parentHandler := controllers.ObjectHandler(controllers.EnqueueForParentHandler(dc.queue, gvk.KubernetesGateway))
   285→
   286→	dc.services = kclient.NewFiltered[*corev1.Service](client, filter)
   287→	dc.services.AddEventHandler(parentHandler)
   288→	dc.clients[gvr.Service] = NewUntypedWrapper(dc.services)
   289→
   290→	dc.configMaps = kclient.NewFiltered[*corev1.ConfigMap](client, filter)
   291→	dc.configMaps.AddEventHandler(controllers.ObjectHandler(func(o controllers.Object) {
   292→		// This could be a configmap referenced by a Gateway paramsRef
   293→		impacted := gatewaysByParamsRef.Lookup(config.NamespacedName(o))
   294→		for _, gw := range impacted {
   295→			dc.queue.AddObject(gw)
   296→		}
   297→		// Or it could also be a global GatewayClass config
   298→		classDefaults, classDefaultsF := o.GetLabels()[gatewayClassDefaults]
   299→		if classDefaultsF && o.GetNamespace() == dc.systemNamespace {
   300→			for _, gw := range dc.gateways.List(metav1.NamespaceAll, klabels.Everything()) {
   301→				if string(gw.Spec.GatewayClassName) == classDefaults {
   302→					dc.queue.AddObject(gw)
   303→				}
   304→			}
   305→		}
   306→	}))
   307→
   308→	dc.deployments = kclient.NewFiltered[*appsv1.Deployment](client, filter)
   309→	dc.deployments.AddEventHandler(parentHandler)
   310→	dc.clients[gvr.Deployment] = NewUntypedWrapper(dc.deployments)
   311→
   312→	dc.serviceAccounts = kclient.NewFiltered[*corev1.ServiceAccount](client, filter)
   313→	dc.serviceAccounts.AddEventHandler(parentHandler)
   314→	dc.clients[gvr.ServiceAccount] = NewUntypedWrapper(dc.serviceAccounts)
   315→
   316→	dc.hpas = kclient.NewFiltered[*autoscalingv2.HorizontalPodAutoscaler](client, filter)
   317→	dc.hpas.AddEventHandler(parentHandler)
   318→	dc.clients[gvr.HorizontalPodAutoscaler] = NewUntypedWrapper(dc.hpas)
   319→
   320→	dc.pdbs = kclient.NewFiltered[*policyv1.PodDisruptionBudget](client, filter)
   321→	dc.pdbs.AddEventHandler(parentHandler)
   322→	dc.clients[gvr.PodDisruptionBudget] = NewUntypedWrapper(dc.pdbs)
   323→
   324→	dc.namespaces = kclient.NewFiltered[*corev1.Namespace](client, filter)
   325→	dc.namespaces.AddEventHandler(controllers.ObjectHandler(func(o controllers.Object) {
   326→		// TODO: make this more intelligent, checking if something we care about has changed
   327→		// requeue this namespace
   328→		for _, gw := range dc.gateways.List(o.GetName(), klabels.Everything()) {
   329→			dc.queue.AddObject(gw)
   330→		}
   331→	}))
   332→
   333→	gateways.AddEventHandler(controllers.ObjectHandler(dc.queue.AddObject))
   334→	gatewayClasses.AddEventHandler(controllers.ObjectHandler(func(o controllers.Object) {
   335→		for _, g := range dc.gateways.List(metav1.NamespaceAll, klabels.Everything()) {
   336→			if string(g.Spec.GatewayClassName) == o.GetName() {
   337→				dc.queue.AddObject(g)
   338→			}
   339→		}
   340→	}))
   341→
   342→	// On injection template change, requeue all gateways
   343→	injectionHandler(func() {
   344→		for _, gw := range dc.gateways.List(metav1.NamespaceAll, klabels.Everything()) {
   345→			dc.queue.AddObject(gw)
   346→		}
   347→	})
   348→
   349→	dc.tagWatcher.AddHandler(dc.HandleTagChange)
   350→
   351→	return dc
   352→}
   353→
   354→func extractListenerSetParent(o *gatewayx.XListenerSet) types.NamespacedName {
   355→	n := o.Spec.ParentRef.Name
   356→	ns := ptr.OrDefault(o.Spec.ParentRef.Namespace, gatewayx.Namespace(o.Namespace))
   357→	return types.NamespacedName{
   358→		Namespace: string(ns),
   359→		Name:      string(n),
   360→	}
   361→}
   362→
   363→func (d *DeploymentController) Run(stop <-chan struct{}) {
   364→	kube.WaitForCacheSync(
   365→		"deployment controller",
   366→		stop,
   367→		d.namespaces.HasSynced,
   368→		d.deployments.HasSynced,
   369→		d.services.HasSynced,
   370→		d.configMaps.HasSynced,
   371→		d.serviceAccounts.HasSynced,
   372→		d.hpas.HasSynced,
   373→		d.pdbs.HasSynced,
   374→		d.gateways.HasSynced,
   375→		d.gatewayClasses.HasSynced,
   376→		d.tagWatcher.HasSynced,
   377→	)
   378→	d.queue.Run(stop)
   379→	controllers.ShutdownAll(
   380→		d.namespaces,
   381→		d.deployments,
   382→		d.services,
   383→		d.configMaps,
   384→		d.serviceAccounts,
   385→		d.hpas,
   386→		d.pdbs,
   387→		d.gateways,
   388→		d.gatewayClasses,
   389→	)
   390→}
   391→
   392→// Reconcile takes in the name of a Gateway and ensures the cluster is in the desired state
   393→func (d *DeploymentController) Reconcile(req types.NamespacedName) error {
   394→	log := log.WithLabels("gateway", req)
   395→
   396→	gw := d.gateways.Get(req.Name, req.Namespace)
   397→	if gw == nil {
   398→		log.Debugf("gateway no longer exists")
   399→		// we'll ignore not-found errors, since they can't be fixed by an immediate
   400→		// requeue (we'll need to wait for a new notification), and we can get them
   401→		// on deleted requests.
   402→		return nil
   403→	}
   404→
   405→	var controller gateway.GatewayController
   406→	if gc := d.gatewayClasses.Get(string(gw.Spec.GatewayClassName), ""); gc != nil {
   407→		controller = gc.Spec.ControllerName
   408→	} else {
   409→		if builtin, f := builtinClasses[gw.Spec.GatewayClassName]; f {
   410→			controller = builtin
   411→		}
   412→	}
   413→	ci, f := classInfos[controller]
   414→	if !f {
   415→		log.Debugf("skipping unknown controller %q", controller)
   416→		return nil
   417→	}
   418→	log.Infof("reconciling gateway with controller %s", ci.controller)
   419→
   420→	// find the tag or revision indicated by the object
   421→	if !d.tagWatcher.IsMine(gw.ObjectMeta) {
   422→		log.Debugf("gateway is not for this revision, skipping")
   423→		return nil
   424→	}
   425→	// TODO: Here we could check if the tag is set and matches no known tags, and handle that if we are default.
   426→
   427→	// Matched class, reconcile it
   428→	return d.configureIstioGateway(log, *gw, ci)
   429→}
   430→
   431→func (d *DeploymentController) configureIstioGateway(log *istiolog.Scope, gw gateway.Gateway, gi classInfo) error {
   432→	// If user explicitly sets addresses, we are assuming they are pointing to an existing deployment.
   433→	// We will not manage it in this case
   434→	if gi.templates == "" {
   435→		log.Debug("skip gateway class without template")
   436→		return nil
   437→	}
   438→	if !IsManaged(&gw.Spec) {
   439→		log.Debug("skip disabled gateway")
   440→		return nil
   441→	}
   442→	existingControllerVersion, overwriteControllerVersion, shouldHandle := ManagedGatewayControllerVersion(gw)
   443→	if !shouldHandle {
   444→		log.Debugf("skipping gateway which is managed by controller version %v", existingControllerVersion)
   445→		return nil
   446→	}
   447→	log.Info("reconciling")
   448→
   449→	var ns *corev1.Namespace
   450→	if d.namespaces != nil {
   451→		ns = d.namespaces.Get(gw.Namespace, "")
   452→	}
   453→	proxyUID, proxyGID := inject.GetProxyIDs(ns)
   454→
   455→	defaultName := getDefaultName(gw.Name, &gw.Spec, gi.disableNameSuffix)
   456→
   457→	serviceType := gi.defaultServiceType
   458→	if o, f := gw.Annotations[annotation.NetworkingServiceType.Name]; f {
   459→		serviceType = corev1.ServiceType(o)
   460→	}
   461→	listenersFromListenerSets := []gateway.Listener{}
   462→	if d.listenerSets != nil {
   463→		sets := d.listenerSetByParent.Lookup(config.NamespacedName(&gw))
   464→		for _, set := range sets {
   465→			if !namespaceAcceptedByAllowListeners(set.Namespace, &gw, func(s string) *corev1.Namespace {
   466→				return d.namespaces.Get(s, "")
   467→			}) {
   468→				continue
   469→			}
   470→			for _, listener := range set.Spec.Listeners {
   471→				s := convertListenerSetToListener(listener)
   472→				listenersFromListenerSets = append(listenersFromListenerSets, s)
   473→			}
   474→		}
   475→	}
   476→
   477→	var nativeSidecarModeEnabled bool
   478→	if features.EnableNativeSidecars == features.NativeSidecarModeDisabled {
   479→		nativeSidecarModeEnabled = false
   480→	} else {
   481→		nativeSidecarModeEnabled = true
   482→	}
   483→
   484→	input := TemplateInput{
   485→		Gateway:        &gw,
   486→		DeploymentName: model.GetOrDefault(gw.Annotations[annotation.GatewayNameOverride.Name], defaultName),
   487→		ServiceAccount: model.GetOrDefault(gw.Annotations[annotation.GatewayServiceAccount.Name], defaultName),
   488→		Ports:          extractServicePorts(gw, listenersFromListenerSets),
   489→		ClusterID:      d.clusterID.String(),
   490→
   491→		KubeVersion:               kube.GetVersionAsInt(d.client),
   492→		Revision:                  d.revision,
   493→		NativeSidecars:            nativeSidecarModeEnabled,
   494→		ServiceType:               serviceType,
   495→		ProxyUID:                  proxyUID,
   496→		ProxyGID:                  proxyGID,
   497→		CompliancePolicy:          common_features.CompliancePolicy,
   498→		InfrastructureLabels:      gw.GetLabels(),
   499→		InfrastructureAnnotations: gw.GetAnnotations(),
   500→		GatewayNameLabel:          label.IoK8sNetworkingGatewayGatewayName.Name,
   501→		IsEastWestGateway:         features.EnableAmbientMultiNetwork && gi.controller == constants.ManagedGatewayEastWestController,
   502→		ControllerLabel:           gi.controllerLabel,
   503→	}
   504→
   505→	// Default to the gateway labels/annotations and overwrite if infrastructure labels/annotations are set
   506→	input.InfrastructureLabels = extractInfrastructureLabels(gw)
   507→	input.InfrastructureAnnotations = extractInfrastructureAnnotations(gw)
   508→	d.setLabelOverrides(gw, input)
   509→
   510→	if overwriteControllerVersion {
   511→		log.Debugf("write controller version, existing=%v", existingControllerVersion)
   512→		if err := d.setGatewayControllerVersion(gw); err != nil {
   513→			return fmt.Errorf("update gateway annotation: %v", err)
   514→		}
   515→	} else {
   516→		log.Debugf("controller version existing=%v, no action needed", existingControllerVersion)
   517→	}
   518→
   519→	rendered, err := d.render(gi.templates, input)
   520→	if err != nil {
   521→		// Just log error, we do not need to retry since rendering errors are not ephemeral errors
   522→		log.Errorf("error rendering templates: %v", err)
   523→		return nil
   524→	}
   525→	for _, t := range rendered {
   526→		if err := d.apply(gi.controller, t); err != nil {
   527→			return fmt.Errorf("apply failed: %v", err)
   528→		}
   529→	}
   530→
   531→	log.Info("gateway updated")
   532→	return nil
   533→}

   535→func (d *DeploymentController) setLabelOverrides(gw gateway.Gateway, input TemplateInput) {
   536→	isWaypointGateway := strings.Contains(string(gw.Spec.GatewayClassName), "waypoint")
   537→	isEastWestGateway := strings.Contains(string(gw.Spec.GatewayClassName), "east-west")
   538→
   539→	var hasAmbientLabel bool
   540→	if _, ok := gw.Labels[label.IoIstioDataplaneMode.Name]; ok {
   541→		hasAmbientLabel = true
   542→	}
   543→	if _, ok := input.InfrastructureLabels[label.IoIstioDataplaneMode.Name]; ok {
   544→		hasAmbientLabel = true
   545→	}
   546→	// If no ambient redirection label is set explicitly, explicitly disable.
   547→	// TODO this sprays ambient annotations/labels all over EVER gateway resource (serviceaccts, services, etc)
   548→	if features.EnableAmbientWaypoints && !isWaypointGateway && !isEastWestGateway && !hasAmbientLabel {
   549→		input.InfrastructureLabels[label.IoIstioDataplaneMode.Name] = constants.DataplaneModeNone
   550→	}
   551→
   552→	// Default the network label for waypoints if not explicitly set in gateway's labels
   553→	network := d.injectConfig().Values.Struct().GetGlobal().GetNetwork()
   554→	if _, ok := gw.GetLabels()[label.TopologyNetwork.Name]; !ok && network != "" && (isWaypointGateway || isEastWestGateway) {
   555→		input.InfrastructureLabels[label.TopologyNetwork.Name] = d.injectConfig().Values.Struct().GetGlobal().GetNetwork()
   556→	}
   557→}

   559→func extractInfrastructureLabels(gw gateway.Gateway) map[string]string {
   560→	return extractInfrastructureMetadata(gw.Spec.Infrastructure, true, gw)
   561→}

   563→func extractInfrastructureAnnotations(gw gateway.Gateway) map[string]string {

   567→func translateInfraMeta[K ~string, V ~string](meta map[K]V) map[string]string {
   568→	infra := make(map[string]string, len(meta))
   569→	for k, v := range meta {
   570→		if strings.HasPrefix(string(k), "gateway.networking.k8s.io/") {
   571→			continue // ignore this prefix to avoid conflicts
   572→		}
   573→		infra[string(k)] = string(v)
   574→	}
   575→	return infra
   576→}
   577→
   578→func extractInfrastructureMetadata(gwInfra *gatewayv1.GatewayInfrastructure, isLabel bool, gw gateway.Gateway) map[string]string {
   579→	if gwInfra != nil && isLabel && gwInfra.Labels != nil {
   580→		return translateInfraMeta(gwInfra.Labels)
   581→	}
   582→	if gwInfra != nil && !isLabel && gwInfra.Annotations != nil {
   583→		return translateInfraMeta(gwInfra.Annotations)
   584→	}
   585→	if !features.EnableGatewayAPICopyLabelsAnnotations {
   586→		return make(map[string]string)
   587→	}
   588→	if isLabel {
   589→		if gw.GetLabels() == nil {
   590→			return make(map[string]string)
   591→		}
   592→		return maps.Clone(gw.GetLabels())
   593→	}
   594→	if gw.GetAnnotations() == nil {
   595→		return make(map[string]string)
   596→	}
   597→	return maps.Clone(gw.GetAnnotations())
   598→}
   599→
   600→const (
   601→	// ControllerVersionAnnotation is an annotation added to the Gateway by the controller specifying
   602→	// the "controller version". The original intent of this was to work around
   603→	// https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/44164, where we needed to transition from a global owner
   604→	// to a per-revision owner. The newer version number allows forcing ownership, even if the other
   605→	// version was otherwise expected to control the Gateway.
   606→	// The version number has no meaning other than "larger numbers win".
   607→	// Numbers are used to future-proof in case we need to do another migration in the future.
   608→	ControllerVersionAnnotation = "gateway.istio.io/controller-version"
   609→	// ControllerVersion is the current version of our controller logic. Known versions are:
   610→	//
   611→	// * 1.17 and older: version 1 OR no version at all, depending on patch release
   612→	// * 1.18+: version 5
   613→	//
   614→	// 2, 3, and 4 were intentionally skipped to allow for the (unlikely) event we need to insert
   615→	// another version between these
   616→	ControllerVersion = 5
   617→)
   618→
   619→// ManagedGatewayControllerVersion determines the version of the controller managing this Gateway,
   620→// and if we should manage this.
   621→// See ControllerVersionAnnotation for motivations.
   622→func ManagedGatewayControllerVersion(gw gateway.Gateway) (existing string, takeOver bool, manage bool) {
   623→	cur, f := gw.Annotations[ControllerVersionAnnotation]
   624→	if !f {
   625→		// No current owner, we should take it over.
   626→		return "", true, true
   627→	}
   628→	curNum, err := strconv.Atoi(cur)
   629→	if err != nil {
   630→		// We cannot parse it - must be some new schema we don't know about. We should assume we do not manage it.
   631→		// In theory, this should never happen, unless we decide a number was a bad idea in the future.
   632→		return cur, false, false
   633→	}
   634→	if curNum > ControllerVersion {
   635→		// A newer version owns this gateway, let them handle it
   636→		return cur, false, false
   637→	}
   638→	if curNum == ControllerVersion {
   639→		// We already manage this at this version
   640→		// We will manage it, but no need to attempt to apply the version annotation, which could race with newer versions
   641→		return cur, false, true
   642→	}
   643→	// We are either newer or the same version of the last owner - we can take over. We need to actually
   644→	// re-apply the annotation
   645→	return cur, true, true
   646→}

   648→type derivedInput struct {
   649→	TemplateInput
   650→
   651→	// Inserted from injection config
   652→	ProxyImage  string
   653→	ProxyConfig *meshapi.ProxyConfig
   654→	MeshConfig  *meshapi.MeshConfig
   655→	Values      map[string]any

   658→func (d *DeploymentController) render(templateName string, mi TemplateInput) ([]string, error) {
   659→	cfg := d.injectConfig()
   660→
   661→	template := cfg.Templates[templateName]
   662→	if template == nil {
   663→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("no %q template defined", templateName)
   664→	}
   665→
   666→	var templateOverlays []map[string]string
   667→
   668→	classConfigs := d.configMaps.List(d.systemNamespace, klabels.SelectorFromValidatedSet(map[string]string{
   669→		gatewayClassDefaults: string(mi.Spec.GatewayClassName),
   670→	}))
   671→	if len(classConfigs) > 0 {
   672→		classConfig := controllers.OldestObject(classConfigs)
   673→		templateOverlays = append(templateOverlays, classConfig.Data)
   674→	}
   675→	params, err := fetchParameters(mi.Gateway)
   676→	if err != nil {
   677→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid parameters: %v", err)
   678→	}
   679→	if params != nil {
   680→		cm := d.configMaps.Get(params.Name, params.Namespace)
   681→		if cm == nil {
   682→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("parametersRef targeting configmap %q, but configmap does not exist", params)
   683→		}
   684→		templateOverlays = append(templateOverlays, cm.Data)
   685→	}
   686→
   687→	labelToMatch := map[string]string{label.IoK8sNetworkingGatewayGatewayName.Name: mi.Name}
   688→	proxyConfig := d.env.GetProxyConfigOrDefault(mi.Namespace, labelToMatch, nil, cfg.MeshConfig)
   689→	input := derivedInput{
   690→		TemplateInput: mi,
   691→		ProxyImage: inject.ProxyImage(
   692→			cfg.Values.Struct(),
   693→			proxyConfig.GetImage(),
   694→			mi.Annotations,
   695→		),
   696→		ProxyConfig: proxyConfig,
   697→		MeshConfig:  cfg.MeshConfig,
   698→		Values:      cfg.Values.Map(),
   699→	}
   700→	results, err := tmpl.Execute(template, input)
   701→	if err != nil {
   702→		return nil, err
   703→	}
   704→
   705→	rawOutput := yml.SplitString(results)
   706→	transformedOutput := make([]string, 0, len(rawOutput))
   707→	for _, output := range rawOutput {
   708→		to, err := applyOverlay(output, templateOverlays)
   709→		if err != nil {
   710→			return nil, err
   711→		}
   712→		if to != "" {
   713→			transformedOutput = append(transformedOutput, to)
   714→		}
   715→	}
   716→	return transformedOutput, nil
   717→}
   718→
   719→var supportedOverlays = sets.New(
   720→	"deployment",
   721→	"service",
   722→	"serviceAccount",
   723→	"horizontalPodAutoscaler",
   724→	"podDisruptionBudget",
   725→)
   726→
   727→var requiredOverlays = sets.New(
   728→	"horizontalPodAutoscaler",
   729→	"podDisruptionBudget",
   730→)
   731→
   732→func applyOverlay(object string, overlaysList []map[string]string) (string, error) {
   733→	var ik crd.IstioKind
   734→	if err := yaml.Unmarshal([]byte(object), &ik); err != nil {
   735→		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to find kind: %v", err)
   736→	}
   737→	gv, err := schema.ParseGroupVersion(ik.TypeMeta.APIVersion)
   738→	if err != nil {
   739→		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to find kind: %v", err)
   740→	}
   741→	kind := &schema.GroupVersionKind{Group: gv.Group, Version: gv.Version, Kind: ik.TypeMeta.Kind}
   742→
   743→	var data any
   744→	var key string
   745→	switch kind.Kind {
   746→	case gvk.Deployment.Kind:
   747→		data = &appsv1.Deployment{}
   748→		key = "deployment"
   749→	case gvk.Service.Kind:
   750→		data = &corev1.Service{}
   751→		key = "service"
   752→	case gvk.ServiceAccount.Kind:
   753→		data = &corev1.ServiceAccount{}
   754→		key = "serviceAccount"
   755→	case gvk.HorizontalPodAutoscaler.Kind:
   756→		data = &autoscalingv2.HorizontalPodAutoscaler{}
   757→		key = "horizontalPodAutoscaler"
   758→	case gvk.PodDisruptionBudget.Kind:
   759→		data = &policyv1.PodDisruptionBudget{}
   760→		key = "podDisruptionBudget"
   761→	default:
   762→		return "", fmt.Errorf("unknown overlay kind %q", kind.Kind)
   763→	}
   764→	applied := false
   765→	for _, overlays := range overlaysList {
   766→		for k := range overlays {
   767→			if !supportedOverlays.Contains(k) {
   768→				return "", fmt.Errorf("unsupported overlay %q (supported: %v)", k, sets.SortedList(supportedOverlays))
   769→			}
   770→		}
   771→		overlay, f := overlays[key]
   772→		if !f {
   773→			continue
   774→		}
   775→		b, err := strategicMergePatchYAML([]byte(object), []byte(overlay), data)
   776→		if err != nil {
   777→			return "", fmt.Errorf("strategic merge patch failed: %v", err)
   778→		}
   779→		applied = true
   780→		object = string(b)
   781→	}
   782→	if !applied && requiredOverlays.Contains(key) {
   783→		return "", nil
   784→	}
   785→
   786→	var finalIK crd.IstioKind
   787→	if err := yaml.Unmarshal([]byte(object), &finalIK); err != nil {
   788→		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to find final kind: %v", err)
   789→	}
   790→
   791→	a, b := ik.ObjectMeta, finalIK.ObjectMeta
   792→	if !(a.Name == b.Name &&
   793→		a.GenerateName == b.GenerateName &&
   794→		a.Namespace == b.Namespace &&
   795→		a.UID == b.UID &&
   796→		a.ResourceVersion == b.ResourceVersion &&
   797→		a.Generation == b.Generation &&
   798→		a.CreationTimestamp == b.CreationTimestamp &&
   799→		a.DeletionTimestamp == b.DeletionTimestamp &&
   800→		a.DeletionGracePeriodSeconds == b.DeletionGracePeriodSeconds &&
   801→		reflect.DeepEqual(a.OwnerReferences, b.OwnerReferences) &&
   802→		slices.Equal(a.Finalizers, b.Finalizers)) {
   803→		return "", fmt.Errorf("illegal metadata change")
   804→	}
   805→	// We could deep equal here but its a bit more tedious, so just never allow setting it
   806→	if len(a.ManagedFields) != 0 || len(b.ManagedFields) != 0 {
   807→		return "", fmt.Errorf("illegal metadata change")
   808→	}
   809→
   810→	return object, nil
   811→}
   812→
   813→// fetchParameters returns the infrastructure parameters for the Gateway. This is currently always a local configmap so we return the name only.
   814→// An error is returned if the parameter is invalid. This does not check the configmap exists, though.
   815→// If no parameter is specified, no name or error is returned.
   816→func fetchParameters(gw *gateway.Gateway) (*types.NamespacedName, error) {
   817→	if gw.Spec.Infrastructure != nil && gw.Spec.Infrastructure.ParametersRef != nil {
   818→		pr := gw.Spec.Infrastructure.ParametersRef
   819→		if string(pr.Kind) == gvk.ConfigMap.Kind && string(pr.Group) == gvk.ConfigMap.Group {
   820→			return &types.NamespacedName{
   821→				Namespace: gw.Namespace,
   822→				Name:      pr.Name,
   823→			}, nil
   824→		}
   825→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown infrastructure parameters type %v/%v", pr.Group, pr.Kind)
   826→	}
   827→	return nil, nil
   828→}

   830→func (d *DeploymentController) setGatewayControllerVersion(gws gateway.Gateway) error {
   831→	patch := fmt.Sprintf(`{"apiVersion":"gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1beta1","kind":"Gateway","metadata":{"annotations":{"%s":"%d"}}}`,
   832→		ControllerVersionAnnotation, ControllerVersion)
   833→
   834→	log.Debugf("applying %v", patch)
   835→	// Use status RBAC so we do not require full Gateway write.
   836→	// `status` write can modify annotations, and we already need to write status anyway so we have the permission.
   837→	return d.patcher(gvr.KubernetesGateway, gws.GetName(), gws.GetNamespace(), []byte(patch), "status")
   838→}

   840→// apply server-side applies a template to the cluster.
   841→func (d *DeploymentController) apply(controller string, yml string) error {
   842→	data := map[string]any{}
   843→	err := yaml.Unmarshal([]byte(yml), &data)
   844→	if err != nil {
   845→		return err
   846→	}
   847→	us := unstructured.Unstructured{Object: data}
   848→	// set managed-by label
   849→	clabel := strings.ReplaceAll(controller, "/", "-")
   850→	err = unstructured.SetNestedField(us.Object, clabel, "metadata", "labels", label.GatewayManaged.Name)
   851→	if err != nil {
   852→		return err
   853→	}
   854→	gvr, err := controllers.UnstructuredToGVR(us)
   855→	if err != nil {
   856→		return err
   857→	}
   858→
   859→	canManage, resourceVersion := d.canManage(gvr, us.GetName(), us.GetNamespace())
   860→	if !canManage {
   861→		log.Debugf("skipping %v/%v/%v, already managed", gvr, us.GetName(), us.GetNamespace())
   862→		return nil
   863→	}
   864→	// Ensure our canManage assertion is not stale
   865→	us.SetResourceVersion(resourceVersion)
   866→
   867→	// Because in 1.24 we removed old label "istio.io/gateway-name", in order to not mutate the deployment.spec.Selector during upgrade.
   868→	// we always use the old `selector` value
   869→	if gvr.Resource == "deployments" {
   870→		deployment := d.deployments.Get(us.GetName(), us.GetNamespace())
   871→		if deployment != nil && deployment.Spec.Selector.MatchLabels["istio.io/gateway-name"] != "" {
   872→			us.Object["spec"].(map[string]any)["selector"] = deployment.Spec.Selector
   873→			// nolint lll
   874→			us.Object["spec"].(map[string]any)["template"].(map[string]any)["metadata"].(map[string]any)["labels"].(map[string]any)["istio.io/gateway-name"] = deployment.Spec.Template.ObjectMeta.Labels["istio.io/gateway-name"]
   875→		}
   876→	}
   877→
   878→	j, err := json.Marshal(us.Object)
   879→	if err != nil {
   880→		return err
   881→	}
   882→	log.Debugf("applying %v", string(j))
   883→	if err := d.patcher(gvr, us.GetName(), us.GetNamespace(), j); err != nil {
   884→		return fmt.Errorf("patch %v/%v/%v: %v", us.GroupVersionKind(), us.GetNamespace(), us.GetName(), err)
   885→	}
   886→	return nil
   887→}

   889→func (d *DeploymentController) HandleTagChange(newTags sets.String) {
   890→	for _, gw := range d.gateways.List(metav1.NamespaceAll, klabels.Everything()) {
   891→		d.queue.AddObject(gw)
   892→	}
   893→}
   894→
   895→// canManage checks if a resource we are about to write should be managed by us. If the resource already exists
   896→// but does not have the ManagedGatewayLabel, we won't overwrite it.
   897→// This ensures we don't accidentally take over some resource we weren't supposed to, which could cause outages.
   898→// Note K8s doesn't have a perfect way to "conditionally SSA", but its close enough (https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/116156).
   899→func (d *DeploymentController) canManage(gvr schema.GroupVersionResource, name, namespace string) (bool, string) {
   900→	store, f := d.clients[gvr]
   901→	if !f {
   902→		log.Warnf("unknown GVR %v", gvr)
   903→		// Even though we don't know what it is, allow users to put the resource. We won't be able to
   904→		// protect against overwrites though.
   905→		return true, ""
   906→	}
   907→	obj := store.Get(name, namespace)
   908→	if obj == nil {
   909→		// no object, we can manage it
   910→		return true, ""
   911→	}
   912→	_, managed := obj.GetLabels()[label.GatewayManaged.Name]
   913→	// If object already exists, we can only manage it if it has the label
   914→	return managed, obj.GetResourceVersion()
   915→}

   937→func extractServicePorts(gw gateway.Gateway, listenerSets []gateway.Listener) []corev1.ServicePort {
   938→	tcp := strings.ToLower(string(protocol.TCP))
   939→	svcPorts := make([]corev1.ServicePort, 0, len(listenerSets)+len(gw.Spec.Listeners)+1)
   940→	svcPorts = append(svcPorts, corev1.ServicePort{
   941→		Name:        "status-port",

Good! I can see that there's a `FilteredObjectSpecHandler` function (line 229-235) that only triggers when the resource spec changes by checking the resourceVersion. This is exactly what we need! The bug is that the deployment controller is using `ObjectHandler` which triggers on ALL updates, including status updates. We need to use `FilteredObjectSpecHandler` instead to ignore status-only updates.

I see the issue now. The `FilteredObjectSpecHandler` uses `ResourceVersion` to detect changes, but that's not correct for detecting spec vs status changes. In Kubernetes:

cat > /testbed/BUG_FIX_SUMMARY.md << 'EOF'
# Bug Fix Summary: Gateway Deployer Running on Status Updates

## Problem

The Gateway deployment controller was running the expensive deployment reconciliation loop on **every** Gateway status update, particularly when the `attachedRoutes` field changed. This field updates frequently as routes are added/removed, causing unnecessary and expensive reconciliations.

## Root Cause

1. **In `deploymentcontroller.go` (line 333)**: The Gateway event handler was using `ObjectHandler`, which triggers on ALL updates including status-only updates.

2. **In `common.go` (line 260)**: The `FilteredObjectSpecHandler` was incorrectly using `GetResourceVersion()` to detect spec changes. In Kubernetes:
   - `ResourceVersion` changes on ANY update (spec OR status)
   - `Generation` only changes when spec is updated (not status)

## Solution

### Change 1: Fix `FilteredObjectSpecHandler` in `pkg/kube/controllers/common.go`

Changed line 260 from:
```go
if onlyIncludeSpecChanges && oldObj.GetResourceVersion() == newObj.GetResourceVersion() {
```

To:
```go
if onlyIncludeSpecChanges && oldObj.GetGeneration() == newObj.GetGeneration() {
```

This ensures that status-only updates (which don't increment Generation) are correctly filtered out.

### Change 2: Use `FilteredObjectSpecHandler` for Gateway events in `deploymentcontroller.go`

Changed lines 333-344 from:
```go
gateways.AddEventHandler(controllers.ObjectHandler(dc.queue.AddObject))
```

To:
```go
// Use FilteredObjectSpecHandler to only trigger reconciliation on spec changes, not status updates
// Status updates (like attachedRoutes) are frequent and should not trigger the expensive deployment loop
gateways.AddEventHandler(controllers.FilteredObjectSpecHandler(dc.queue.AddObject, func(o controllers.Object) bool {
    return true // Accept all objects, but only on spec changes (generation change)
}))
```

This ensures that only spec changes trigger the deployment reconciliation, not status updates.

### Change 3: Fix test to handle Generation

Updated `deploymentcontroller_test.go` to manually increment Generation when updating Gateway specs, since the fake Kubernetes client doesn't do this automatically (but real Kubernetes does).

## Impact

- **Before**: Every status update (including frequent `attachedRoutes` changes) triggered a full deployment reconciliation
- **After**: Only spec changes trigger deployment reconciliation; status updates are ignored
- **Result**: Significant reduction in unnecessary reconciliation loops, improving performance and reducing API server load

## Testing

All existing tests pass:
- `TestConfigureIstioGateway`: ✓
- `TestVersionManagement`: ✓ (updated to handle Generation properly)
- All other gateway package tests: ✓
- All controller package tests: ✓

## Files Modified

1. `/testbed/pkg/kube/controllers/common.go` - Fixed Generation check
2. `/testbed/pilot/pkg/config/kube/gateway/deploymentcontroller.go` - Use filtered handler
3. `/testbed/pilot/pkg/config/kube/gateway/deploymentcontroller_test.go` - Fixed test Generation handling

## Kubernetes Generation vs ResourceVersion

For future reference:
- **Generation**: Monotonically increasing counter that increments ONLY when the desired state (spec) changes
- **ResourceVersion**: String that changes on ANY update to the object (spec or status)
- **Best Practice**: Use Generation to detect spec changes, not ResourceVersion
EOF

## Summary

I have successfully fixed the bug "Gateway deployer runs deployment loop on each Gateway status update". Here's what was done:

### The Problem
The Gateway deployment controller was running expensive reconciliation loops on every Gateway status update, particularly when the `attachedRoutes` field changed frequently. This was causing unnecessary API server load and performance issues.

### Root Cause
1. The gateway event handler in `deploymentcontroller.go` was using `ObjectHandler` which triggers on ALL updates
2. The `FilteredObjectSpecHandler` in `common.go` was incorrectly using `GetResourceVersion()` instead of `GetGeneration()` to detect spec changes

### The Fix

**Three files were modified:**

1. **`pkg/kube/controllers/common.go`** - Fixed the `FilteredObjectSpecHandler` to use `GetGeneration()` instead of `GetResourceVersion()` to properly detect spec-only changes (line 260)

2. **`pilot/pkg/config/kube/gateway/deploymentcontroller.go`** - Changed the gateway event handler from `ObjectHandler` to `FilteredObjectSpecHandler` so that only spec changes trigger reconciliation (lines 333-337)

3. **`pilot/pkg/config/kube/gateway/deploymentcontroller_test.go`** - Updated the test to manually increment Generation on spec updates, since the fake Kubernetes client doesn't do this automatically (7 locations)

### Impact
- **Before**: Every status update triggered full reconciliation (expensive)
- **After**: Only spec changes trigger reconciliation (efficient)
- Status updates like `attachedRoutes` changes no longer cause unnecessary reconciliation

### Testing
✅ All existing tests pass:
- `TestVersionManagement` 
- `TestConfigureIstioGateway`
- All gateway package tests
- All controller package tests

The fix correctly distinguishes between spec updates (which should trigger reconciliation) and status updates (which should not), using the Kubernetes-standard `Generation` field.
